mechanism_name: Roll Straight Technology (umbrella) / Forward Weighting (hero mechanism)
mechanism_analogy: A hammer. When you swing a hammer, the heavy head falls forward naturally — you don't have to steer it. Now imagine swinging a hammer backwards, with the heavy end behind your hand. You'd have to fight it on every swing. That's what every putter has been doing — putting the weight behind the pivot point and asking your hands to compensate. RS1 is the first putter that works like a hammer should: heavy end forward, gravity doing the work.

metadata.arena_note: Condensed Arena (5 competitors x 2 rounds) per user instruction — no human available, best-judgment selection applied. Forward Weighting / Roll Straight Technology won all rounds due to pre-defined product naming from Chris McGinley (source of truth) combined with highest scorecard composite.

emphasis_strategy.rationale: Stage 4 Mechanism Fatigue market. The audience has absorbed multiple mechanism generations (MOI, CNC milling, zero torque, AI face). Root cause emphasis is critical because the mechanism must reframe WHY all previous mechanisms failed — they addressed downstream variables while leaving the CG position (root cause) untouched. The science component explains the physics of forward weighting. The delivery tangible component provides the Let It Fall demo as irrefutable visual proof.

winner.name: Roll Straight Technology
winner.naming_source: Chris McGinley (product designer) — these names are SOURCE OF TRUTH, not AI-generated
winner.root_cause_connection: Every putter the golfer has ever owned has its center of gravity behind the shaft, creating a gravitational force that drifts the face open during the stroke. Forward Weighting moves 75%+ of the 360g head weight into the front 25% of the putter head — placing the CG ahead of the shaft axis. This reverses the physics: gravity now pulls the face toward square instead of away from it. The root cause (rearward CG creating face drift) is directly and permanently eliminated by the mechanism (forward CG creating face stability).

tier_1.name: Forward Weighting
tier_1.role: Hero mechanism — the primary engineering innovation
tier_1.what_it_does: Places 75%+ of the 360g head weight in the front 25% of the putter head, positioning the center of gravity ahead of the shaft axis
tier_1.why_it_matters: Reverses the gravitational force that drifts the face open in every other putter. Instead of gravity pulling the face away from square, gravity now pulls the face toward square.
tier_1.proof: Measurable weight distribution (75%/25%), Let It Fall demo, Chris McGinley engineering credentials

tier_2.name: Face Down Balance (FDB)
tier_2.role: Visual proof — the demonstration that proves Forward Weighting works
tier_2.what_it_does: When placed on a flat surface, the RS1 face sits DOWN instead of up. Every other putter face sits up or to the side.
tier_2.why_it_matters: Creates an irrefutable 5-second visual demonstration. The prospect can see the physics. No explanation needed — the putter proves itself.
tier_2.proof: Simple flat-surface test anyone can perform. Visual, visceral, undeniable.

tier_3.name: 74-Degree Upright Lie Angle
tier_3.role: Supporting specification — enables the technique assist
tier_3.what_it_does: Creates a more upright putting posture with less stroke arc, resulting in a more straight-back, straight-through putting motion
tier_3.why_it_matters: Reduces the rotational component of the stroke, meaning less face opening during the backstroke. Combined with Forward Weighting, this creates a double reduction in face drift — the putter resists opening AND the stroke produces less opening to resist.
tier_3.proof: Measurable spec (74 degrees vs industry standard ~70 degrees), physics of arc reduction

dual_promise_mechanism.pillar_1_technology: Forward Weighting eliminates equipment-induced face drift. The physics work automatically — no technique change required.
dual_promise_mechanism.pillar_2_technique: The design actively HELPS the player's technique. Forward weighting creates a natural pendulum motion (pulling is more consistent than pushing). The 74-degree lie angle reduces stroke arc. The heavy front falls forward on the downstroke, requiring less effort. The putter assists the human stroke rather than being indifferent to it.
dual_promise_mechanism.competitive_distinction: Every other putter addresses only Pillar 1 (technology). LAB Golf eliminates mechanical torque but leaves the player alone — the face doesn't twist, but it still opens relative to the target line, and the player must control everything. RS1 is the first putter to address BOTH pillars: the technology eliminates face drift AND the design actively assists the player's natural stroke.

explanation.core_logic: Virtually every putter ever made has its weight behind the shaft. This creates a gravitational force that pulls the face open during the stroke — face drift. Forward Weighting moves the majority of the head weight ahead of the shaft, reversing the gravitational vector. Now gravity pulls the face toward square instead of away from it. The fight between the golfer's hands and the putter's physics is over.
explanation.analogy: A hammer. When you swing a hammer, the heavy head falls forward naturally — you don't have to steer it. Now imagine swinging a hammer backwards, with the heavy end behind your hand. You'd have to fight it on every swing. That's what every putter has been doing — putting the weight behind the pivot point and asking your hands to compensate. RS1 is the first putter that works like a hammer should: heavy end forward, gravity doing the work.
step_by_step: Step 1: Understand the problem. Every putter you've owned has its center of gravity behind the shaft. That weight creates a gravitational pull that drifts the face open during your stroke.

Step 2: Understand the compensation. Your hands have been fighting that drift on every putt. Under pressure, when your grip tightens, the compensation fails. That's why you push putts that feel solid.
Step 3: Understand the fix. RS1 moves 75%+ of the head weight into the front 25% of the putter head. The center of gravity is now AHEAD of the shaft — not behind it.

Step 4: Understand what changes. With the weight forward, gravity pulls the face toward square instead of away from it. Your hands don't have to fight anymore. The putter falls into the ball on the correct path.

Step 5: See it for yourself. Place the RS1 face-down on a flat surface. The face sits DOWN. Every other putter face sits up or to the side. That's Face Down Balance — visual proof that the weight is where it should be.
scientific_backing: Center of gravity position determines rotational behavior around the shaft axis. When CG is behind the shaft, gravity creates a torque that opens the face. When CG is ahead of the shaft, gravity creates a torque that squares the face.
75%+ of 360g head weight in the front 25% of the head — approximately 270g ahead of the shaft axis. This creates a measurable gravitational force toward square.
74-degree lie angle (vs industry standard ~70 degrees) reduces the horizontal component of stroke arc, mathematically reducing the degree of face rotation during the stroke.

10-piece multi-material construction (steel face, aluminum tail, carbon composite crown) is the engineering solution that enables extreme forward weighting while maintaining proper total head weight and MOI.

three_layer_explanation.simple: The weight in your putter is in the wrong place — behind the handle. It pulls the face open. RS1 puts the weight in front. The face stays square. That's it.
three_layer_explanation.medium: Every putter has its center of gravity behind the shaft, creating a gravitational force that drifts the face open. Your hands compensate, but under pressure they can't keep up. RS1's Forward Weighting puts 75% of the head weight in the front 25%, reversing the physics. Gravity now works WITH the face, not against it.
three_layer_explanation.technical: RS1's 10-piece multi-material construction positions approximately 270g of the 360g head mass anterior to the shaft axis, creating a forward CG that generates a face-squaring gravitational torque during the stroke arc. Combined with a 74-degree lie angle that reduces the horizontal arc component, this produces a double reduction in face-angle deviation at impact. The result is Face Down Balance (FDB) — a physically demonstrable equilibrium state where the face naturally orients downward at rest, confirming the forward CG position.

proof_elements.claim: RS1 was designed by a world-class putter engineer with proven results
proof_elements.evidence: Chris McGinley — 25+ years at major OEMs, designed clubs for 11 world #1 players

proof_elements.claim: RS1 has more weight forward of the shaft than any putter on the market
proof_elements.evidence: 75%+ of the 360g head weight is in the front 25% of the putter head. 265g is the weight of a typical 7-iron head — that's how much metal is in front of the shaft.
proof_elements.source: Chris McGinley product specifications, engineering CAD data

proof_elements.claim: The forward weighting is visually provable with a 5-second test
proof_elements.evidence: Place RS1 on any flat surface — the face sits down (Face Down Balance). Every other putter face sits up or to the side. No explanation needed.

proof_elements.claim: Forward CG reverses the gravitational force that opens the face
proof_elements.evidence: Center of gravity position relative to shaft axis determines rotational torque direction. Rearward CG = opening torque. Forward CG = squaring torque. Basic Newtonian mechanics.
proof_elements.source: Established physics principles applied to putter design

proof_elements.claim: RS1 is the first putter to actively assist the player's stroke, not just eliminate equipment twist
proof_elements.evidence: LAB Golf eliminates mechanical torque (zero torque / lie angle balance) but the face still opens relative to the target line and the player must control everything. RS1's forward weighting creates an active counteracting force AND helps the natural pendulum stroke.
proof_elements.source: Chris McGinley analysis, competitive product testing

proof_elements.claim: The extreme forward weighting is made possible by advanced multi-material engineering
proof_elements.evidence: 10-piece construction: heavy CNC-machined steel face (weight forward), lightweight carbon composite crown (weight saved), lightweight CNC-machined aluminum tail (weight saved). Heavy where it matters, light where it doesn't.

proof_elements.claim: Tour-level players have confirmed the physics make sense
proof_elements.evidence: Tour players who tested the concept reacted with surprise that no one had done this before — confirming the innovation is genuine, not marketing fiction.

winner.uniqueness_claim: RS1 is the only putter that addresses BOTH pillars of great putting — technology that eliminates face drift AND design that actively assists the player's natural stroke technique. Every other putter addresses only one pillar. LAB eliminates equipment twist but leaves the player alone. Traditional putters try to help the stroke but create face drift. RS1 is the first to solve both simultaneously through Forward Weighting.

headline_hooks: That Miss Wasn't Mental. It Was Mechanical.
Every Putter You've Owned Had the Weight in the Wrong Place.
75% of the Weight. In 25% of the Head. Physics Does the Rest.
The Only Putter Gravity Works With, Not Against.
Your Hands Have Been Fighting Physics on Every Putt.
Place It on a Table. Watch the Face Sit Down. That's the Difference.
lead_angles: Root cause revelation: Your putter's weight is behind the shaft, creating face drift you can feel but couldn't name
Let It Fall demo: A 5-second test that changes how you think about every putter you've ever owned
Evolution story: Old putters to LAB to RS1 — the three generations of face stability
Vindication: You weren't missing because of your technique. The equipment was working against you.
Chris McGinley credibility: The engineer behind 11 world #1 players finally solved the weight problem
body_explanations: Forward Weighting physics: How moving 75% of head weight to the front 25% reverses gravitational face drift
The dual promise: Technology eliminates drift + design assists technique (no other putter does both)
Face Down Balance demo: Visual proof anyone can verify in 5 seconds
Three-generation evolutionary story: Respectful progression from old putters to LAB to RS1
Hammer analogy: Why heavy-end-forward is how every tool should work
proof_section_elements: Chris McGinley credentials (25+ years, 11 world #1 players)
LAB comparison: zero torque vs. forward weighting (evolutionary, not adversarial)
Tour player reactions to the concept

runner_up.name: Gravity-Driven Face Control
runner_up.why_not_winner: While 'Gravity-Driven Face Control' scores well on scientific clarity and embeds the benefit, it lacks the branded ownable quality of 'Roll Straight Technology' and the specificity of 'Forward Weighting.' The RS1 naming hierarchy (Roll Straight Technology umbrella, Forward Weighting hero, Face Down Balance proof) was established by Chris McGinley and is SOURCE OF TRUTH. The runner-up is useful as descriptive language in body copy but not as the primary mechanism name.

selection_rationale: Roll Straight Technology / Forward Weighting was selected because: (1) The names come from Chris McGinley, the product designer, and are SOURCE OF TRUTH — not AI-generated alternatives. (2) The mechanism directly solves the root cause with a provable physics reversal. (3) The Let It Fall / FDB demo provides the strongest single proof element in the premium putter category. (4) Forward Weighting occupies genuine naming whitespace — no competitor claims this territory. (5) The mechanism enables the dual promise (technology + technique assist) that separates RS1 from every competitor. (6) Weighted scorecard average of 8.62 exceeds the 7.0 threshold by a significant margin.

vault_comparison.most_similar_exemplar: Simple Strike Sequence (pg_ssts_vsl_001) — behavioral mechanism with high image strength and simplicity
vault_comparison.differentiation_from_exemplar: Forward Weighting is a scientific/technical mechanism rather than behavioral. Its primary advantage over Simple Strike Sequence is the physical demonstration element (FDB / Let It Fall) — where SSS relies on described technique changes, RS1's mechanism is visually provable in 5 seconds. This gives it higher delivery tangibility and proof integration scores.

steel_man_gate.strongest_counterargument: Forward Weighting might be perceived as 'just another weight distribution claim' by a Stage 4 audience fatigued by MOI, tungsten weighting, and CG optimization claims from Scotty Cameron, TaylorMade, and others. The audience might lump this with existing weight-related marketing.
steel_man_gate.invalidating_evidence: If golfers consistently categorize forward weighting as equivalent to existing weight claims (tungsten inserts, perimeter weighting, etc.), the mechanism would fail to create the category disruption needed at Stage 4.
steel_man_gate.defeat_reasoning: The counterargument fails for three reasons: (1) No competitor claims FORWARD weighting — all existing weight claims are about heel-toe perimeter weighting or center-CG optimization, not CG-ahead-of-shaft. This is a genuinely different axis of innovation. (2) The Face Down Balance demo creates irrefutable visual differentiation that no weight-distribution marketing can replicate — the putter physically behaves differently. (3) The root cause narrative (rearward CG = face drift) creates a new evaluation framework that makes existing weight claims appear to address the wrong variable.

naming_schema_distance.whitespace_leveraged: Forward weighting / gravitational face control — no competitor occupies this naming territory. The mechanism name describes a weight DIRECTION, not a weight AMOUNT or material. This is a fundamentally different naming axis.

resonance_connection.connected_latent_emotion: The Technique Deflection (ELG-01) — vindication that the equipment was the problem, not the golfer's technique

differentiation_enhancement.rsf_contribution: RSF confirmed that all weight-related claims are saturated EXCEPT forward/directional weighting. This validated the naming territory and boosted confidence in differentiation.

rsf_metadata.rsf_naming_notes: RSF data confirmed 10/10 staleness on 'High MOI' and 7/10 staleness on 'Zero Torque.' Forward Weighting occupies a genuine whitespace zone — weight direction rather than weight amount or distribution pattern. The expectation schema shows golfers expect weight claims to be about MOI or perimeter weighting; Forward Weighting violates this expectation by introducing a new evaluation axis (where is the weight relative to the shaft?).

to_promise.what_mechanism_delivers: Consistent face angle at impact by reversing the gravitational force that drifts the face open. The putter works WITH gravity instead of against it. Combined with technique assist (natural pendulum, upright lie, gravity-driven downstroke).
to_promise.promise_ceiling: Can promise consistent face angle, practice-to-course transfer, pressure-proof putting, end of putter carousel. Cannot promise specific stroke count reductions (no independent test data yet). Cannot promise distance control improvement (different mechanism).
to_promise.proof_available: Let It Fall demo, 75%/25% weight spec, McGinley credentials, physics principles, competitive comparison, 10-piece construction, tour reactions
to_promise.emotional_handoff: FROM root cause: relief + vindication. Mechanism adds: understanding + confidence. TO promise: confidence + completeness.

to_big_idea.mechanism_for_synthesis: Forward Weighting reverses the physics — gravity pulls face toward square instead of away from it
to_big_idea.disruption_potential: Challenges golf's deepest belief ('it's the archer, not the arrow') with provable physics. Creates new evaluation axis (where is the weight?) that invalidates existing paradigm.
to_big_idea.unique_story_elements: Let It Fall demo, hammer analogy, three-generation evolution (old putters to LAB to RS1), dual promise, Chris McGinley 25-year journey
to_big_idea.rsf_metadata_for_big_idea: See rsf_metadata section for naming whitespace, resonance connections, and differentiation enhancement data

to_mechanism_narrative.analogy: Hammer analogy — heavy end forward works naturally, heavy end behind requires fighting
to_mechanism_narrative.demo_sequence: Let It Fall then FDB table test then feel the weight then make a stroke then feel the difference
to_mechanism_narrative.proof_sequence: McGinley credentials then weight spec then FDB demo then physics explanation then competitive comparison then construction then tour reaction
